stops.name: Konak Square & Clock Tower
stops.shortDescription: The heart of Izmir. The beautiful Moorish-style Clock Tower was built in 1901 to commemorate the 25th anniversary of Sultan Abdülhamid II's accession to the throne.

stops.name: Konak Yalı Mosque
stops.shortDescription: A tiny, perfectly octagonal 18th-century mosque located right next to the Clock Tower. It is elegantly decorated with beautiful Kütahya tiles around its windows.

stops.name: Kemeraltı Bazaar Entrance
stops.shortDescription: The entrance to one of the oldest and largest open-air bazaars in the world. Dating back to the 17th century, its winding streets are packed with everything from bridal wear to fresh spices.

stops.name: Kızlarağası Hanı
stops.shortDescription: A stunningly restored 18th-century caravanserai. The inner courtyard is a favorite local spot to sit down, rest your feet, and enjoy a cup of traditional Turkish coffee brewed over hot sand.

stops.name: Hisar Mosque
stops.shortDescription: Izmir's oldest and grandest mosque, built in 1597. The incredibly ornate interior features a massive central dome supported by eight massive pillars and stunning Islamic calligraphy.

stops.name: Havra Sokağı (Synagogue Street)
stops.shortDescription: The historic Jewish quarter of the bazaar. Today it is a vibrant, chaotic alleyway packed with fishmongers, cheese vendors, and fruit stalls, offering a true taste of Izmir's culinary diversity.

stops.name: Agora of Smyrna
stops.shortDescription: The impressive ruins of the ancient Roman market, originally built by Alexander the Great and later rebuilt by Marcus Aurelius. You can walk through its incredibly well-preserved subterranean arched vaults.

stops.name: Çankaya Square
stops.shortDescription: A busy transition point bridging the ancient, winding streets of the Kemeraltı Bazaar and the more modern, wide grid streets of downtown Izmir.

stops.name: Mimar Kemalettin Fashion Street
stops.shortDescription: A beautifully pedestrianized street lined with elegant, early 20th-century Neoclassical buildings. It is famously known as the wedding dress and high fashion capital of the city.

stops.name: Cumhuriyet Square
stops.shortDescription: The wide, open waterfront 'Republic Square', featuring an imposing equestrian statue of Mustafa Kemal Atatürk leading the Turkish War of Independence.

stops.name: Pasaport Pier
stops.shortDescription: A historic ferry terminal built in 1876 in the French architectural style. The seawall in front of the pier is a favorite spot for locals to sit on the grass and watch the ferries cross the bay.

stops.name: Arkas Art Center
stops.shortDescription: Housed in the beautiful historic French Honorary Consulate building overlooking the sea. It hosts world-class rotating exhibitions featuring major works by international and Turkish artists.

stops.name: Gündoğdu Square
stops.shortDescription: A massive, grassy seaside square defined by the 'Tree of the Republic' monument. It is the primary location for massive open-air concerts and city-wide celebrations in Izmir.

stops.name: Alsancak Kordon
stops.shortDescription: Izmir's iconic, sweeping waterfront promenade. Lined with palm trees, cycling paths, and seafood restaurants, it is the absolute best place in the city to catch a spectacular Aegean sunset.
